name: Java Collections Framework Hierarchy
chatResponse: I'll guide you through the structural hierarchy of the Java Collections Framework, illustrating how core interfaces relate to their concrete implementations.

steps.title: The Root Interfaces
steps.content: The framework is divided into two main branches: the `Collection` hierarchy and the `Map` hierarchy. Everything begins with the `Iterable` interface, which allows objects to be the target of the 'for-each' loop statement.

steps.title: The Collection Branch
steps.content:
The `Collection` interface is the parent of three major sub-interfaces:
- **List**: Ordered collections that allow duplicates (e.g., `ArrayList`, `LinkedList`).
- **Set**: Unordered collections that prohibit duplicates (e.g., `HashSet`, `TreeSet`).
- **Queue**: Designed for holding elements prior to processing, usually in FIFO order (e.g., `PriorityQueue`, `ArrayDeque`).

steps.title: The Map Hierarchy
steps.content: Unlike other collections, `Map` does not extend `Collection`. It stores data as **Key-Value pairs**. Common implementations include `HashMap` (using hashing) and `TreeMap` (sorted by keys).

steps.title: Choosing the Right Implementation
steps.content:
Performance varies by structure:
| Interface | Implementation | Access | Search | Insert |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| List | ArrayList | O(1) | O(n) | O(n) |
| Set | HashSet | N/A | O(1) | O(1) |
| Map | HashMap | O(1) | O(1) | O(1) |

visualCode:
graph TD
    Iterable["Iterable (Interface)"] --> Collection["Collection (Interface)"]
    
    Collection --> List["List (Interface)"]
    Collection --> Set["Set (Interface)"]
    Collection --> Queue["Queue (Interface)"]
    
    List --> ArrayList["ArrayList (Class)"]
    List --> LinkedList["LinkedList (Class)"]
    List --> Vector["Vector (Class)"]
    
    Set --> HashSet["HashSet (Class)"]
    Set --> LinkedHashSet["LinkedHashSet (Class)"]
    Set --> SortedSet["SortedSet (Interface)"]
    SortedSet --> TreeSet["TreeSet (Class)"]
    
    Queue --> PriorityQueue["PriorityQueue (Class)"]
    Queue --> Deque["Deque (Interface)"]
    Deque --> ArrayDeque["ArrayDeque (Class)"]
    
    Map["Map (Interface)"] --> HashMap["HashMap (Class)"]
    Map --> Hashtable["Hashtable (Class)"]
    Map --> LinkedHashMap["LinkedHashMap (Class)"]
    Map --> SortedMap["SortedMap (Interface)"]
    SortedMap --> TreeMap["TreeMap (Class)"]
